title: Raisin Bran Muffins
description: The taste of raisin bran cereal in the texture of a muffin.
source_attribution: Based on the original from Pinch of Yum
steps: Whisk milk, oil, yogurt, and applesauce in a medium bowl.
Add cereal and mix until fully coated and mostly submerged.
Let sit for 20 minutes (collect other ingredients and tools).
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Grease a 12-cup muffin tin.
Fully mix in eggs.
Mix in raisins if using.
Gently sprinkle flours across the top of the batter to make a dry layer, then sprinkle baking soda and salt over the flours.
Fold until just combined.
Distribute batter between muffin cups with an ice cream scoop for good tops.
Sprinkle a little brown sugar on top of each muffin.
Bake 20 minutes, until a tester comes out clean.
Cool in the pan 10 minutes, then remove and cool completely.

title: Ginger Bran Muffins with Citrus
description: Ginger-forward bran muffins with a citrus slice baked into each muffin. Adapted from Billie's Gingerbread Cake (Ruth Reichl's Delicious) and Smitten Kitchen sour cream bran muffins.

ingredients.name: Orange
ingredients.prep_note: small, peeled and sliced ⅛–¼ inch for filling

steps: Grind peppercorns, cloves, cardamom, and cinnamon individually; measure ½ tsp peppercorns, ½ tsp cloves, ½ tsp cardamom, and ¾ tsp cinnamon into a medium bowl.
Peel and grate ginger. Zest citrus, then peel and slice oranges into ⅛–¼ inch rounds.
Butter or spray a muffin tin. Preheat oven to 400°F.
Beat butter with brown sugar until fluffy. Add honey, then egg, then yogurt, scraping the bowl between additions. Mix in zest and ginger.
Add pastry flour, bran, baking soda, and salt to the spice bowl; whisk and add to the wet mixture. Mix until just combined.
Fill each cup about a quarter full with batter. Add a citrus slice and press gently. Add another layer of batter and a second citrus slice on top.
Bake at 400°F for 12 minutes. Gently press top citrus slices back into muffins and test with a toothpick; bake 5 minutes more if needed.
Cool 5 minutes in the pan, then turn out gently (inner citrus slice can make them fragile).

title: English Muffins: Whole Wheat
description: Hearty whole-wheat English muffins adapted from NYT. Griddled then finished in the oven.
steps: Pour ⅓ cup warm (not hot) water over yeast; stir and let sit 5+ minutes until fragrant.
Mix yogurt, milk, olive oil, maple syrup, and salt until combined.
Sift together flours and baking soda.
Stir yeast mixture into wet ingredients, then add dry ingredients. Mix with a wooden spoon (or hands) until flour is incorporated.
Cover and rise about 1½ hours until doubled and airy.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment and cornmeal.
Heat a cast-iron skillet over medium. Oil a ¼-cup measure and fingers.
Scoop dough portions into the skillet, spread gently into disks, cover, and cook 1 minute on medium-high then 3 minutes on medium-low. Flip and repeat.
Transfer griddled muffins to the prepared sheet. Repeat until you have about 10 muffins.
Bake 10 minutes at 350°F. Cool at least 5 minutes before splitting and toasting.

title: Pistachio Shortbread with Rose Buttercream
description: Pistachio shortbread with cocoa nibs and lemon zest, topped with rose buttercream. A farewell cookie for Callie — pale green shortbread with pink roses was the goal.
story: Callie offhandedly mentioned loving shortbread with cacao nibs — this cookie pairs creamy butter, bitter nibs, and rose buttercream.
steps: Toast pistachios at 350°F until fragrant, 6–9 minutes; cool. Grind with ¼ cup flour in a food processor.
Beat butter and sugar until creamy. Add vanilla. Sift remaining flour, salt, and baking powder; mix until just combined.
Stir in nut mixture, nibs, and lemon zest. Form a disk, wrap, and chill at least 3 hours.
For rose buttercream: whip butter, add 1 tbsp milk, rosewater, and coloring. Add powdered sugar until frosting consistency; chill 10 minutes before piping.
Heat oven to 325°F. Roll dough to ⅛ inch (squares) or shape 1-inch disks (rounds). Space on parchment.
Bake about 12 minutes, rotating trays halfway, until lightly browned. Cool on a rack.
Pipe rose buttercream on cooled cookies if using.
